acara_description: Represent three-dimensional objects in 2 dimensions; discuss the advantages and disadvantages of different representations.

questions.stem: How many faces does a cube have?

default: Count the flat surfaces: top, bottom, front, back, left, right.
A cube has 6 identical square faces.
6 faces.

questions.stem: How many faces does a triangular prism have?

default: Count: 2 triangular ends + 3 rectangular sides.
2 triangles + 3 rectangles = ?
5 faces.

questions.worked_solution: Triangular prism: 2 triangular faces + 3 rectangular faces = 5 faces.

questions.stem: A cube's net is made up of:

default: A net folds up to form the solid.
A cube has 6 faces, so its net needs 6 squares.
6 squares.

questions.worked_solution: A cube has 6 faces. Its net has 6 squares.

questions.stem: What does the top view (bird's-eye view) of a cylinder look like?

default: Imagine looking straight down at a cylinder.
The circular top is what you see.
Circle.

questions.worked_solution: Viewed from above, a cylinder shows its circular cross-section.

questions.stem: How many vertices (corners) does a rectangular prism have?

default: A rectangular prism is like a box.
Count the corners: there are 4 on top and 4 on the bottom.
4 + 4 = 8 vertices.

questions.stem: How many edges does a triangular prism have?

default: Count edges on each triangle (3 each) and edges joining them (3).
Triangle 1: 3 edges. Triangle 2: 3 edges. Connecting edges: 3.

questions.worked_solution: A triangular prism has 9 edges: 3 on each triangular face + 3 along the length.

questions.stem: The front view of a square pyramid looks like:

default: Imagine looking at a pyramid straight from the front.
You see the triangular face facing you.
Triangle.

questions.worked_solution: The front view of a pyramid is a triangle.

questions.stem: Which of the following can be the net of a cube?
options: Some arrangements of 6 squares, but not all

answer.correct: Some arrangements of 6 squares, but not all

default: Not all arrangements of 6 squares fold into a cube.
Some configurations leave gaps or overlap when folded.
There are 11 valid nets of a cube.

questions.worked_solution: Of all possible arrangements of 6 squares, only 11 fold into a valid cube.

questions.stem: An isometric drawing is used to:
options: Draw the exact dimensions of a 2D shape
Show a 3D object on a triangular grid
Draw only the top view of a 3D shape
Show a cross-section through a solid

answer.correct: Show a 3D object on a triangular grid

default: Isometric means equal measures.
It uses a triangular dot grid to make 3D shapes look realistic.
Three axes are shown at equal angles.

questions.worked_solution: Isometric drawings use a triangular grid to represent 3D objects in 2D.

questions.stem: How many faces does a square pyramid have?

default: Count: 1 square base + triangular sides.
A square base has 4 edges, so there are 4 triangular faces.
1 + 4 = 5 faces.

questions.worked_solution: Square pyramid: 1 square base + 4 triangular faces = 5 faces.
